sourceMovie.title: Wedding Crashers
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Wedding Crashers for the same reason it broke through in 2005: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a romance rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. David Dobkin keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Bride Wars
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Wedding Crashers clicked for you, Bride Wars (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Wedding Crashers?
faq.answer: Try Bride Wars, My Best Friend's Wedding, Runaway Bride—each shares DNA with Wedding Crashers while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Wedding Crashers?
faq.answer: Wedding Crashers (2005) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Wedding Crashers worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
